title: Fix single-pipe typo in @nestjs/core peerDependency range
description: package.json line 56 has '"@nestjs/core": "^10 | ^11"' (single pipe). npm semver only recognises '||' (double pipe) as the OR operator; a single pipe is not a valid range operator and will produce an unintended range. Change to '^10 || ^11' to match the @nestjs/common peer entry above it.

title: Handle puppeteer-extra global plugin state — dedupe + document caveat
description: lib/puppeteer-core.module.ts:45 calls puppeteer.use(plugin) per module options. puppeteer-extra is a global singleton so plugins from different forRoot() calls bleed across browsers. Repeated module re-registration in tests stacks plugins. At minimum: dedupe (skip if already registered) and add a JSDoc warning on PuppeteerModuleOptions.plugins. Investigate per-browser scoping if puppeteer-extra exposes a non-singleton API.

title: Verify and fix multi-browser shutdown closes every Browser instance
description: lib/puppeteer-core.module.ts:103-114 onApplicationShutdown resolves 'getBrowserToken(this.options)' from its own moduleRef. With multiple PuppeteerCoreModule instances, each instance closes only its own browser. Add a multi-browser e2e spec asserting both browsers actually disconnect on app close. Fix any leak found.
notes: Investigation: added tests/e2e/puppeteer-multi-browser.spec.ts covering both forRoot and forRootAsync with two distinct browser instances. Verified via browser.process().pid that Puppeteer spawns separate Chromium processes per instance and that browser.connected flips to false on app.close() for both. No leak found: each PuppeteerCoreModule instance receives its own scoped PUPPETEER_MODULE_OPTIONS, so getBrowserToken(this.options) in onApplicationShutdown resolves the correct per-instance browser token. Closing as verified-by-test.
close_reason: Verified by new e2e spec; per-instance shutdown works correctly.

title: Tighten test suite: cut redundancy, fix flaky externals, fill coverage gaps
description: Audit-driven cleanup of tests/e2e/ per /home/venom/.claude/plans/can-you-go-over-peaceful-petal.md. Cuts: redundant async-config spec, third-party URL test, duplicate rebrowser case, tautological asserts. Adds: token-helper unit-style spec, useClass+name-threading spec, named-browser forFeature assertion, happy-path shutdown logging. Saves ~30-60s/CI cell across the 4-cell matrix.
acceptance_criteria: all e2e specs green; build & lint pass; net Chromium boot count reduced; tokens spec runs <1s
close_reason: Suite tightened per audit plan: dropped puppeteer-async-config (redundant), replaced external bot-detection URL with self-hosted goto, dropped tautological asserts, added token-helper spec (16 cases), useClass+name-threading spec, named-browser forFeature assertion, happy-path shutdown logging. 8 spec files / 33 tests, full suite green in 4.2s.

title: Make puppeteer an optional peer; require either puppeteer or rebrowser-puppeteer
description:
## Context

`nestjs-puppeteer` declares `puppeteer ^22 || ^23 || ^24` as a **required** peer dependency and `rebrowser-puppeteer ^24` as **optional**. That arrangement does not match how the library is meant to be used: a consumer who wants `rebrowser-puppeteer` (via the `launcher` option) is forced to also install `puppeteer`, which doubles the Chromium download for nothing. The hard requirement comes from `lib/puppeteer-core.module.ts:12` — `import puppeteer, { Browser } from 'puppeteer';` — the eager default-import resolves the package at module load time, even when the consumer always passes `options.launcher`.

The goal is to make **either** `puppeteer` **or** `rebrowser-puppeteer` satisfy the install (at least one must be present), following the canonical NestJS pattern used by `@nestjs/microservices`, `@nestjs/jwt`, `@nestjs/swagger`, etc.: mark backends optional in `peerDependenciesMeta` and lazy-load them with the first-party `loadPackage` utility from `@nestjs/common`.

## Design

NestJS ships `loadPackage(packageName, context, loaderFn?)` at `@nestjs/common/utils/load-package.util` (see `node_modules/@nestjs/common/utils/load-package.util.js`). It tries `require()` (or `loaderFn()`) and on failure logs `The "<name>" package is missing. Please, make sure to install it to take advantage of <context>.` then `process.exit(1)`. This is exactly the helper to use here.

**Resolution rule** at the runtime call site (`forRoot` and `forRootAsync` `useFactory`):

1. If `options.launcher` is set → use it. Never touch `puppeteer`.
2. Else → `loadPackage('puppeteer', 'PuppeteerModule', () => require('puppeteer'))` and use the resolved default export. If `puppeteer` is missing, `loadPackage` logs and exits with the friendly NestJS error.

There is **no auto-fallback to `rebrowser-puppeteer`**. A consumer who wants rebrowser must pass it as `launcher` explicitly. This keeps behaviour predictable and matches the existing public API contract.

**DI token decision (settled):** `getBrowserToken` (`lib/common/puppeteer.utils.ts:6-16`) keeps using the upstream `Browser` *class* as the token for the default unnamed browser. Consumers who run `rebrowser-puppeteer` only must `npm install puppeteer-core` (Chromium-free) to obtain the `Browser` value/type for `app.get(Browser)` and `import { Browser } from 'puppeteer'`-shaped use. This avoids a public-API break and is consistent with the existing `CLAUDE.md` note about `Browser` class identity.

## Out of scope

- Switching the default DI token from the `Browser` class to a string. Filed as future work if needed; would require its own design and a clear deprecation path.
- Auto-fallback from `puppeteer` to `rebrowser-puppeteer` when `puppeteer` is missing. Explicit `launcher` is the only supported way to use rebrowser.
- `puppeteer-extra` re-introduction. Removed in v3.0.0 per the existing README note; not coming back.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `package.json` marks both `puppeteer` and `rebrowser-puppeteer` optional in `peerDependenciesMeta`.
- [ ] `lib/puppeteer-core.module.ts` no longer eagerly imports `puppeteer` as a value at module top-level. The runtime fallback uses `loadPackage`.
- [ ] `lib/puppeteer.providers.ts` and `lib/interfaces/puppeteer-options.interface.ts` use `import type`.
- [ ] `lib/common/puppeteer.utils.ts` continues to value-import `Browser` (DI token); a source comment documents why.
- [ ] `npm run build` and `npm run test:e2e` pass on a clean checkout with `puppeteer` installed.
- [ ] `npm run test:e2e` passes for `puppeteer-rebrowser.spec.ts` specifically (launcher-passed path).
- [ ] README documents the three install variants and updates the `[!IMPORTANT]` note about `Browser` to mention `puppeteer-core`.
- [ ] Commit uses `feat:` with `BREAKING CHANGE:` footer (or `feat!:`).
notes:
## Files to change

### 1. `package.json` — peerDependenciesMeta

```diff
   "peerDependenciesMeta": {
+    "puppeteer": { "optional": true },
     "rebrowser-puppeteer": { "optional": true }
   }
```

Both backends become optional from npm's standpoint. Enforcement of "at least one is reachable" is delegated to `loadPackage` at runtime.

### 2. `lib/puppeteer-core.module.ts` — replace eager import with lazy `loadPackage`

Current:

```ts
import puppeteer, { Browser } from 'puppeteer';
// ...
useFactory: async (options: PuppeteerModuleOptions) => {
  return await (options.launcher ?? puppeteer).launch(options);
},
```

Target:

```ts
import { loadPackage } from '@nestjs/common/utils/load-package.util';
import type { Browser, PuppeteerNode } from 'puppeteer';
// ...
useFactory: async (options: PuppeteerModuleOptions) => {
  const launcher =
    options.launcher ??
    (loadPackage(
      'puppeteer',
      'PuppeteerModule',
      () => require('puppeteer'),
    ) as PuppeteerNode);
  return await launcher.launch(options);
},
```

Apply in both `forRoot` (~line 73) and `forRootAsync` (~line 100). Hoist into a small `resolveLauncher(options)` helper if you want to avoid duplication. `Browser` becomes `import type` (only used as a generic at line ~118 in `onApplicationShutdown`).

`loadPackage` is exported from `@nestjs/common/utils/load-package.util`. It is also re-exported from `@nestjs/common`'s public surface in current versions — verify against `@nestjs/common@^10` (the lowest supported peer) and prefer the deep path if either fails to resolve there.

### 3. Flip remaining puppeteer imports to `import type`

- `lib/puppeteer.providers.ts:2` — `import { Browser } from 'puppeteer';` → `import type { Browser } from 'puppeteer';`
- `lib/interfaces/puppeteer-options.interface.ts:7` — `import { Browser, PuppeteerNode } from 'puppeteer';` → `import type { Browser, PuppeteerNode } from 'puppeteer';`
- `lib/common/puppeteer.utils.ts:2` — **leave `Browser` as a value import** (it is the runtime DI token at lines 10 and 14). `Page` can flip to `import type`. Add a one-line source comment explaining why `Browser` stays as a value import so future readers do not "fix" it.

### 4. README — document the install variants

Update `## Compatibility` to reflect that `puppeteer` is optional, and add a short table or paragraph for the three valid setups:

| Setup                               | Install                                                                |
| ----------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Vanilla puppeteer                   | `npm i nestjs-puppeteer puppeteer`                                     |
| rebrowser-puppeteer (with launcher) | `npm i nestjs-puppeteer rebrowser-puppeteer puppeteer-core`            |
| Custom launcher                     | `npm i nestjs-puppeteer <your-launcher> puppeteer-core` (for `Browser`) |

Update the existing `## Notes` `[!IMPORTANT]` about `Browser` import to explicitly mention `puppeteer-core` as the recommended source for rebrowser-only setups.

### 5. (Optional) e2e spec for the missing-puppeteer path

Realistic to skip — `loadPackage` calls `process.exit(1)` on miss, which is hard to assert in jest without spawning a child process. The existing `tests/e2e/puppeteer-rebrowser.spec.ts` already exercises the `launcher`-passed branch (the only branch that does *not* hit `loadPackage`), so coverage is sufficient. If reviewers ask, spawn a subprocess with `NODE_PATH` pointing at a temp `node_modules` lacking `puppeteer` and assert exit code 1 + the `PackageLoader` log on stderr.

## Critical files

- `package.json` — peerDependenciesMeta
- `lib/puppeteer-core.module.ts` — lazy `loadPackage` in both `useFactory`s; `import type { Browser }`
- `lib/puppeteer.providers.ts` — `import type`
- `lib/interfaces/puppeteer-options.interface.ts` — `import type`
- `lib/common/puppeteer.utils.ts` — keep `Browser` value import (intentional, comment it)
- `README.md` — install variants + DI-token note tweak

## Reusing existing utilities

- `loadPackage` from `@nestjs/common` is the canonical NestJS lazy-package loader. Used by `@nestjs/microservices` (per-transport), `@nestjs/jwt`, `@nestjs/swagger`. Do **not** hand-roll a try/catch; use this.
- The existing `launcher`-aware `useFactory` logic at `puppeteer-core.module.ts:73,100` is the only place that needs runtime puppeteer access. No other call site in `lib/` invokes a puppeteer function.

## Verification

```sh
npm run lint && npm run build       # baseline sanity
npm run test:e2e                    # full suite, both puppeteer and rebrowser specs
```

Targeted lazy-load check (manual):

```sh
node -e '
  const { PuppeteerModule } = require("./dist");
  const dyn = PuppeteerModule.forRoot({
    launcher: { launch: () => Promise.resolve({ close() {} }) },
  });
  console.log("ok: forRoot with launcher constructed without resolving puppeteer");
'
```

CI: the `test-libs` matrix (NestJS 10/11 × Puppeteer 23/24, with `rebrowser-puppeteer ^24` always present) continues to pass — that proves the puppeteer-present path still works. Optionally add a matrix cell that runs `npm uninstall puppeteer` after `npm clean-install` and runs only `puppeteer-rebrowser.spec.ts` to prove the rebrowser-only path; skip if the implementer judges it noisy.

## Commit message

`feat:` with a `BREAKING CHANGE:` footer (or `feat!:`) — making `puppeteer` optional alters the install contract for consumers and warrants a major bump under semantic-release.

title: CI matrix across NestJS 10/11 and Puppeteer 23/24
description: pull_request.yml currently only varies node-version (20/22/24) and runs against the dev-pinned @nestjs/* ^11 and puppeteer ^24. We declare support for @nestjs/* ^10 || ^11 and puppeteer ^22 || ^23 || ^24, plus optional rebrowser-puppeteer ^24, but never test those combinations. Expand the test job matrix to cover NestJS 10 and 11 against Puppeteer 23 and 24, with rebrowser-puppeteer ^24 always present.

title: Document supported puppeteer/NestJS versions in README
description: README does not currently list which puppeteer (v22/v23/v24), NestJS (10/11), or rebrowser-puppeteer (v24) versions the package supports. Users have to read package.json peerDependencies. Add a Compatibility / Supported versions section.

title: Add launcher option for swapping puppeteer backend
description: Add an optional launcher field to PuppeteerModuleOptions that lets users pass a drop-in puppeteer alternative (primary target: rebrowser-puppeteer). Keeps existing forRoot/forRootAsync/forFeature shape - no new module classes, no new statics. Adds rebrowser-puppeteer as optional peer dep + devDep (single Chromium download via shared cache).

title: Open PR for puppeteer/v24 branch and verify semantic-release major bump
description:
After both feat commits land on the puppeteer/v24 branch, open the PR against master and validate the release pipeline:

- CI matrix (Node 20/22/24 under aa-exec --profile=chrome) must be green.
- Public API surface check: node -e "const m=require('./dist'); console.log(Object.keys(m).sort())" must list everything except PUPPETEER_BROWSER_PLUGINS and PuppeteerModuleOptions.plugins.
- semantic-release dry-run output in CI logs should announce a MAJOR bump driven by the two BREAKING CHANGE footers (v3.0.0).
- PR description should call out the two breaks for downstream users: peer dep narrowed to ^22||^23||^24 (drop v21, add v24); puppeteer-extra plugin path removed (pin to 2.x branch if needed).

This is the consumer-facing deliverable; until the PR merges and master ships, the branch work is not done.
notes:
PR #56 open: https://github.com/oblakstudio/nestjs-puppeteer/pull/56
CI status (run 25601300148):
- Lint: success
- Test Node.js 20 / 22 / 24 (aa-exec --profile=chrome): success
- Coverage: skipped (gated on event_name == merge_group; expected)
Public API surface verified locally — PUPPETEER_BROWSER_PLUGINS removed.
Awaiting merge so the master Release workflow runs semantic-release and cuts v3.0.0 from the BREAKING CHANGE footer.

title: feat(deps)!: drop puppeteer-extra integration
description:
Remove the puppeteer-extra plugin code path entirely. puppeteer-extra (and puppeteer-extra-plugin-stealth) have not published since 2023-03-01 (~3 years); Snyk's advisor flags the project as inactive. Stealth evasions target old-headless and have not been re-tuned for the new-headless default introduced in puppeteer v22. Community has moved to rebrowser-puppeteer for serious stealth.

This is a public-API break (drops PuppeteerModuleOptions.plugins, PUPPETEER_BROWSER_PLUGINS constant, puppeteer-extra peer dep). Users who still need plugins are pointed at the 2.x branch in the README.

Files:
- package.json: remove peerDependencies.puppeteer-extra; remove devDependencies.puppeteer-extra and devDependencies.puppeteer-extra-plugin-stealth.
- lib/puppeteer-core.module.ts: import puppeteer from 'puppeteer' (not 'puppeteer-extra'); delete PuppeteerExtraPlugin import, pluginRegistrationLogger, registerPlugins helper, PUPPETEER_BROWSER_PLUGINS import, and the pluginProvider blocks in forRoot/forRootAsync.
- lib/puppeteer.constants.ts: delete PUPPETEER_BROWSER_PLUGINS export.
- lib/interfaces/puppeteer-options.interface.ts: delete PuppeteerExtraPlugin import and the plugins?: PuppeteerExtraPlugin[] field with its JSDoc.
- tests/e2e/puppeteer-with-plugin.spec.ts: delete file.
- tests/src/app-with-plugin.module.ts: delete file.
- tests/src/base/base.controller.ts: delete the @Get('stealth-check') handler (the only consumer of arh.antoinevastel.com bot test).
- tests/e2e/puppeteer.spec.ts: delete the PUPPETEER_DISABLE_HEADLESS_WARNING env-flag line (v21 deprecation warning, no longer emitted on v22+).
- README.md: drop puppeteer-extra from install command; remove plugins row from options table; add callout pointing legacy users at the 2.x branch and recommending rebrowser-puppeteer for new projects.

Verification:
- npm install && npm run build && npm run lint && npm run test:e2e (6 remaining specs).
- grep -r puppeteer-extra lib/ tests/ README.md → zero matches.
- grep -r PUPPETEER_BROWSER_PLUGINS lib/ tests/ → zero matches.
- grep -r PuppeteerExtraPlugin lib/ tests/ → zero matches.
- Diff dist/index.d.ts exports vs baseline: only PUPPETEER_BROWSER_PLUGINS and PuppeteerModuleOptions.plugins should disappear.

Commit message: feat(deps)!: drop puppeteer-extra integration
With BREAKING CHANGE footer.

title: feat(deps): bump puppeteer peer range to ^22 || ^23 || ^24
description:
Drop puppeteer ^21 from the peer-dep range and add v24 (currently latest 24.43.0, GA 2025-01-10), so the supported range becomes ^22 || ^23 || ^24. Also bump the dev dep to puppeteer ^24 and refresh stale docs that still reference removed APIs.

The library is already structurally insulated (uses Parameters<PuppeteerNode['launch']>[0] and only Browser/Page instance APIs), so no logic changes are needed — but README and JSDoc still show headless: 'new' (gone since v22) and PuppeteerNodeLaunchOptions (renamed to LaunchOptions in v24). Those have to be corrected for type-safety on v24.

Files:
- package.json: peerDependencies.puppeteer ^21||^22||^23 → ^22||^23||^24; devDependencies.puppeteer ^23 → ^24; refresh package-lock.json.
- lib/interfaces/puppeteer-options.interface.ts: update the LaunchOptions JSDoc range; refresh the plugins-block headless comment (headless: 'new' no longer exists; for puppeteer-extra plugin compat use headless: 'shell').
- README.md: replace 'New Headless' callout with current-state note; replace every headless: 'new' with headless: true; replace every PuppeteerNodeLaunchOptions with LaunchOptions.

Verification:
- npm install && npm run build && npm run lint && npm run test:e2e (all 7 specs incl. stealth-check).
- npx tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.json (sanity-check launch-options derivation on v24).

Commit message: feat(deps): bump puppeteer peer range to ^22 || ^23 || ^24
With BREAKING CHANGE footer noting v21 drop and headless: 'new' / PuppeteerNodeLaunchOptions removal.

title: Tune up CI workflows: release.yml + pull_request.yml
description:
Both GitHub Actions workflows have drifted from current repo layout and modern CI practice.

release.yml is broken at job-time:
- runs npm test (no such script in package.json)
- npm clean-install in test/ directory which does not exist
- extra_plugins: @semantic-release/github duplicates plugin already in .releaserc
- triggers on beta and *.x branches that .releaserc does not configure

pull_request.yml drifted:
- Node 21 in matrix (EOL)
- job named 'test' actually only does lint and audit; lint depends on test_matrix unnecessarily
- push: branches: [renovate/**] trigger duplicates the pull_request trigger Renovate already fires
- coverage re-runs full Chromium e2e on every PR just to upload Codecov

Decisions (confirmed with user):
- Trust merge queue; release runs build + publish only
- Trim release branches to master + next
- Node matrix: 20, 22, 24
- Coverage only on merge_group

Plan file: ~/.claude/plans/i-did-some-changes-mellow-hare.md

title: Audit major dependency bumps flagged by ncu
description:
Several deps have major-version bumps available. Each needs review of breaking changes and CI impact before applying:

- @commitlint/cli, @commitlint/config-angular: 18 → 21
- @types/jest: 29 → 30 (paired with jest 30)
- @types/node: ^20 → ^25
- @types/supertest: 6 → 7 (paired with supertest 7)
- @typescript-eslint/eslint-plugin, @typescript-eslint/parser: 6 → 8
- eslint: 8 → 10 (flat config migration required)
- eslint-config-prettier: 9 → 10
- jest: 29 → 30
- lint-staged: ^15 → ^17
- supertest: 6 → 7
- typescript: ^5 → ^6

NOTE: puppeteer ^23 → ^24 is being handled separately and is NOT in scope for this issue.

Applied safe bumps in nestjs-puppeteer-qim.

title: Dependency sanity check and bumps
description: Audit dependencies, devDependencies, and peerDependencies in package.json against the current registry. Identify outdated packages (npm outdated). For each: classify as safe-bump (patch/minor) vs major (needs review). Apply safe bumps in one commit; flag majors with a short note for the user to decide. Re-run lint, build, and e2e after bumping. Do NOT bump peerDependency ranges without confirming the public-API impact.

title: Validate forRootAsync requires one of useFactory/useClass/useExisting
description: lib/puppeteer-core.module.ts:145-147 type-asserts (options.useClass || options.useExisting) as Type — if all three of useFactory/useClass/useExisting are missing, the inject array contains undefined and DI fails with a confusing error. Throw an explicit 'PuppeteerModule.forRootAsync requires one of useFactory, useClass, or useExisting' early.

title: Tighten public types — drop any from forRoot and async options
description: lib/puppeteer-core.module.ts:35 has 'forRoot(options: any = {})' — should be 'PuppeteerModuleOptions'. lib/interfaces/puppeteer-options.interface.ts:33-34 has 'useFactory?: (...args: any[])' and 'inject?: any[]' — should match @nestjs/typeorm convention using InjectionToken[] / OptionalFactoryDependency. Pure type-tightening, no runtime change.

title: Library quirk: forRootAsync(name) requires factory to also return name in options
description: puppeteer-core.module.ts:128-129 looks up the browser at shutdown via getBrowserToken(this.options) — but for forRootAsync({useClass|useFactory}) with a named registration, this.options is whatever the factory returned. If the factory omits name, shutdown looks up the default Browser token instead of '$nameBrowser', and moduleRef.get throws. Existing tests work around this by duplicating name in both the registration and the factory return (see puppeteer-multi-browser.spec.ts:54-56). Possible fix: capture options.name at registration time and prefer it over this.options.name in onApplicationShutdown.
notes: Discovered while writing puppeteer-async-useclass.spec.ts. Test currently follows the duplication convention to keep moving.

title: Modernize puppeteer launch-options type for v23 compatibility
description: lib/interfaces/puppeteer-options.interface.ts uses Partial<PuppeteerNodeLaunchOptions>. Puppeteer v23 renamed this to LaunchOptions. Pick a type strategy that works across the peer-dep range '^21 || ^22 || ^23' without breaking consumers. Verify in a clean install against each major.

title: Detect duplicate browser name registration
description: lib/puppeteer-core.module.ts:54,84 registers a browser provider keyed by getBrowserToken(options). Two forRoot() calls with the same 'name' silently overwrite the DI token — no warning. Throw a clear error or log a warning when a duplicate name is detected (track registered names in a module-level Set).

title: Improve onApplicationShutdown error logging (preserve stack)
description: lib/puppeteer-core.module.ts:111-113 currently logs 'e?.message' only — drops stack and breaks for non-Error throws. Pass the full error to this.logger.error so the Nest logger can format it properly.

value: rebrowser-puppeteer's published install.mjs is broken: it imports downloadBrowsers from upstream puppeteer (puppeteer/internal/node/install.js) which uses upstream's pinned Chromium revision instead of rebrowser's own. As a result npm install leaves rebrowser without its pinned chrome and .launch() fails with 'Could not find Chrome (ver. X)'. Workaround: import from rebrowser-puppeteer/internal/node/install.js (rebrowser's own internal entry) and call downloadBrowsers() — this exists as a subpath export. Used in scripts/install-rebrowser-chrome.mjs as pretest:e2e hook.

User's preferred handoff workflow: when a non-trivial task is scoped during a conversation, write the implementation plan into a beads issue (bd create with --description, --notes, --acceptance) so they can /clear the conversation and execute the bead in a fresh session. The bead must be self-contained — fresh-Claude has to be able to execute from 'bd show <id>' alone, without conversation context. Pattern: scope task → ask design questions → bd create with full plan → user clears → next session implements from the bead.

User preference: do NOT engineer for single-Chromium install across puppeteer + rebrowser-puppeteer in this repo. They are fine with both packages downloading their own Chromium revision separately. Quote: 'we do not have to have single executable - they can differ. fuck ci download speeds'. Apply when designing install/test setup involving multiple puppeteer flavors.
